task: Write a Python function balanced(s) returning True when the parentheses in the string are balanced, considering only the characters '(' and ')' and ignoring everything else. A closing parenthesis must never appear before its opening partner: ')(' is not balanced. An empty string is balanced. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly checks for balanced parentheses in the input string.
trace: The function `balanced(s)` takes a string `s` as input and uses a stack data structure to check the balance of parentheses. Each time an opening parenthesis '(' is encountered, it is added to the stack. When a closing parenthesis ')' appears, if the stack is empty or the top element in the stack is not an opening parenthesis, the function returns `False`. The function returns `True` only when there are no unmatched parentheses and the stack is empty at the end. An edge case handled here is when the input string contains extra non-parenthesis characters.
